meaning: n.狂妄自大者,大摇大摆,吹牛vi.昂首阔步,虚张声势吓人,大摇大摆,吹牛vt.吓唬,恫吓adj.漂亮的,时髦的
examples: He walked with something of a swagger.
Plenty of South Asians resent India's swagger.
All this brave talk might seem easy to dismiss as the swagger of an arrogant upstart.
His cautious take aside, big money is back in venture capital--and so's the swagger.
"Keith Richards' Before They Make Me Run arrives with its screw-you swagger intact, " he added.
